lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I received your message my liege. How may I serve the kingdom?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Ahh... adventurer. The repair work on the Well of Voyage has been completed. We're finally ready to move against my brother.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I assume you have a plan?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I do indeed. I recently managed to make contact with the elves that inhabit the western lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elves? I’ve never seen an elf before.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Well that may well change soon. Apparently, my brother has been causing them quite a bit of trouble. As such, they've agreed to help us get rid of him once and for all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I need to do?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: You are to head into the Underground Pass and through the Well of Voyage. Lord Iorwerth, the leader of the elves, will send some representatives to meet you on the other side.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: With their help, you are to find my brother and kill him. Once that's done, return to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well. Anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: My brother may not be the only threat you face. I hear the elves are currently dealing with a rebellion. The rebels likely won't take kindly to you working with their enemy so be on the look out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I'll be careful.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Good luck adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't think I'm ready for this yet.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: As you wish. Return to me when you are ready and we will begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the deal with your brother again?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: My brother, King Tyras, was always a bit of an explorer. He went on numerous expeditions to the lands west of here. On one of these expeditions, he was captured by the forces of the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: The Dark Lord agreed to spare his life, but only on one condition... That he would drink from the Chalice of Eternity. The chalice corrupted him. He joined forces with the Dark Lord, the embodiment of pure evil…

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: As a result, I had a wall built around West Ardougne to act as a barrier between him and the rest of the world. Tyras is king of West Ardougne, sealing off the city was the only way to stop him and the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I knew people would never believe the truth, so I had the plague made up as an excuse for the cordon. I'm not proud of it, but it was the only way to keep people safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But now we’re ready to stop him.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Indeed we are. My brother has been gathering strength in the west, preparing to move against us. But now that we have a way through, we can move against him first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excellent. Do you have a plan in place?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I do indeed. I recently managed to make contact with the elves that inhabit the western lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. See you later.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Farewell adventurer. Return to me once you are ready to begin.

lines.speaker: Idris
lines.text: Halt human! What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Idris
lines.text: Wait! What was that? What the...

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: Are you the human by the name of [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes that's me.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Good... We've been expecting you. King Lathas sent word of your coming.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: You should speak with Lord Iorwerth. He will help you track down that brigand Tyras. You'll find his camp in the north west of the forest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, I'm [player name]. Your scouts said that I should come and see you.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Ahh... [player name]. Welcome to Tirannwn. I understand you've been sent here to rid us of that criminal Tyras. I offer my full support.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Unfortunately, most of my troops are currently dealing with a group of renegade elves. That does mean we're a bit more limited than we otherwise would be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I'll need to play this smart then.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Indeed. Now, Tyras and his forces moved camp recently so your first step will be to find their new camp. I have a tracker currently working in that area. I suggest you join up with him.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: You'll find him at Tyras's old camp. It's south east of here, just north of that poisoned lake. Once you've found the camp, come and let me know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I shall return when my task is complete.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: Human! You must be one of Tyras's men...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No I'm [player name]! Lord Iorwerth said you might be able to help me.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: And you have something to prove this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... Err... No.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: As I was saying... I have no time for brigands or outlaws.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Good day [player name]. How may I help you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your scout refused to help. He asked for proof that you sent me.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Bless his loyalty but curse his suspicion. Here he will recognise this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: Human! You must be one of Tyras's men...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No I'm [player name]! Lord Iorwerth said you might be able to help me.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: And you have something to prove this?

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: That's Lord Iorwerth's pendant. He must have a lot of faith in you. Now, what is it I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to find Tyras and kill him. Do you know where his camp is?

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: Well this was his old camp. After the battle a few days ago they moved. We're yet to find them again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help at all?

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: As it goes I'm not actually tracking them at the moment. I'm currently trying to trace our renegade brethren instead. This here is the best lead we've found so far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is?

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: Ahh I guess you can't see it with those human eyes.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: I tell you what. Now that you're here I may as well give you a hand. I'll search here on the east side. You check out the west end of the camp. Come and tell me if you find anything.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: [player name], how goes your search? Any luck hunting down that brigand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found no trace of him as yet.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I'm sure you will find something at his old camp that can help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take a look and see what I can find.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Good. Come find me when you've located the camp.

sections.title: Speaking with the Elf Tracker again before finding the tracks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing again?

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: Check out the west end of the camp and see if you can find anything. I'll keep looking here. Let me know if you find anything.

sections.title: Speaking with the Elf Tracker again after finding the tracks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found tracks leading off to the west. But they trail off into the trees. Beyond that I am unable to follow.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: These forests aren't always as dense as you'd think. If you look closer, you might see ways that you can get through. With that in mind, why don't you give it another go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks... I'll see what I can find.

sections.title: Returning to the Elf Tracker after finding the camp

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: How goes the hunt for that bandit camp?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found it a short distance west of here.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: I'm sure Lord Iorwerth will be pleased to hear that. You should let him know.

lines.speaker: Tyras Guard
lines.text: I see you making friends with that elf, traitor!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: [player name] how goes your search... Any luck hunting down that brigand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've finally tracked the brute down. His camp is in a secluded bit of forest, almost directly south of here.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Good job. As we don't have the troops available to take on his forces directly, I suggest we try some alternative tactics.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I have this book here which details an explosive reaction. We should be able to use it to create a bomb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that should make short work of Tyras.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Indeed. Have a read through and see what you think. Once you have, let me know if you have any questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost my copy of The Big Book of Bangs.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Its lucky I had a scribe make a few copies then, is it not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

sections.title: Speaking with Lord Iorwerth about ingredients

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Good day [player name]. Have you had any luck with that book?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some quicklime.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Quicklime?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently it's made by heating limestone.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Ah, I see. There's a mine directly east of here where you can mine limestone.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some sulphur.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Check the shore south of the old camp you were at earlier. I've seen sulphur there in the past, it comes from that poisoned lake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good, thanks.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some naphtha.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Naphtha?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: According to the book, you get it through the fractional distillation of coal tar.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Well you should be able to get some coal tar from that poisoned lake to the south. As for the rest, I'm afraid I don't have the chemistry knowledge to understand it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I know someone from Ardougne who might be able to help. She knows a thing or two about chemistry.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Perfect! Take the book to her and see if she can help. Just don't tell her what it's for. Even if she's trustworthy, we can't take any risks.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Anyway, is there anything else I can help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a barrel.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Have a look around the camp, there should be some empty barrels lying around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do, thanks.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a fuse.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Some sort of fabric should work. You can use the loom here if needed.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all thanks.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Let me know if you need anything else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day. I was hoping you could help me with something. I have a book which mentions the fractional distillation of coal tar. I don't know how to do that though.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Ah, you'll be wanting to use the still then. It's just outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I use it?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: It's quite simple really. You have to get the still heat up very high for it to work. However, if it gets too hot it will burn out and you will have to start over.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: You must also watch the pressure, if it gets too high you will also have to start over. There are valves to control pressure and tar flow. These will also affect the heat of the still.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that all?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: You'll also need plenty of coal to keep the still hot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, thanks for the information.

sections.title: Returning to Lord Iorwerth before using the bomb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Good day [player.] Have you had any luck with that book?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have finished the bomb.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Excellent work. Now we can finally deal with Tyras.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what should I do? Plant it in his camp?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: No, you'll be spotted by the guards if you try that. You'll need to get it in from outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any suggestions?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Tyras and his men have used catapults against us in the past. Maybe you can turn one of them against him.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Now you'd better get going. Don't forget, you'll need a tinderbox to light that fuse. Return to me once the deed is done.

sections.title: Trying to use the catapult before distracting the guard

lines.speaker: Tyras guard
lines.text: Oi! Don't mess with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Tyras guard
lines.text: Yeah, what ya want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering what you were doing out here?

lines.speaker: Tyras guard
lines.text: I was just wondering what I was doing here too. I'm meant to be looking after this catapult but I can't keep my mind on the job. We've been living off field rations for almost a year now.

lines.speaker: Tyras guard
lines.text: I keep seeing rabbits in this forest. What I wouldn't give to eat one, but I'm not quick enough to kill any and I'm no good with a bow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see how that would be distracting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I have one here for you.

lines.speaker: Tyras guard
lines.text: You cooked me a rabbit! Thanks a lot. You don't mind watching things while I take five to eat it, do you?

sections.title: Talking to the Guard after killing Tyras

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Tyras guard
lines.text: They say the king died from a flying fire ball. Are you certain no one messed with the catapult while I was eating?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was the only one here. I was watching the whole time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: I take it that the huge explosion I just heard was you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've finally dealt with Tyras, he should cause you no more problems.

lines.speaker: Elf Tracker
lines.text: This is good news indeed. Lord Iorwerth will be very happy. You should go and let him know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth, it is done.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Good good... One of my scouts reported that they saw his tent in a ball of flames. You have done well.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I'm sure you will want to get back to your king and tell him. He will undoubtedly want some proof. He should trust me enough in this matter, Give him this letter, it verifies that the job is done.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: As a token of my own thanks, I give you access to the pass through Arandar. You'll find the start of the trail in the far north east of the woods.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you my lord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth, I have lost the message for King Lathas.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Here have another copy, but don't let it fall into the wrong hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you my lord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Have you delivered the message to King Lathas yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet my lord.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: There is no time for delay, he'll need to see it.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Are you the human by the name [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's me?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Thank Seren, maybe all is not yet lost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I am Arianwyn, leader of the Elven Resistance. We've been watching you since you arrived in Tirannwn. We know it was you that murdered King Tyras.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: There is much to explain and little time. You carry a message from Lord Iorwerth, do you not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you seem to know everything else so I'll not lie. Yes.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Good, we understand each other well. Now hand it here and find the truth of who your real employer is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had no idea! Why would the king do this?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I have a few ideas, now is not the time to discuss them though. For now, you should go to King Lathas as normal. Give him the message and act like nothing unusual has happened.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Once you are done, return to Tirannwn and seek out Islwyn and Eluned. You'll find them roaming the Isafdar forest. They will bring you to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to help you? Okay, what's in it for me?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: The chance for redemption after all you've done? If that doesn't take your fancy, consider this...

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: This isn't a struggle for land or title. It is a war for life, all life. If Iorwerth and Lathas manage to summon Zamorak, we all die.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Deliver your message, act normal. We'll meet again soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My lord, Tyras is dead!

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: This is grand news indeed! You'll forgive me if I'm a little apprehensive about this, but I take it you do have proof?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I have nothing to corroborate this.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I am sure you have done as you say, but on this matter I can afford no doubts. You must find something to confirm your story.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have a letter sent by Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Yes... Good... This will do nicely. Well done, you've done an excellent job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does this mean we can reveal the truth about the plague?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Not yet I'm afraid. Even with my brother gone, the Dark Lord remains a threat. For now, the plague must remain.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Anyway, I'll send word when I next require your services. Oh, and I guess you'll be wanting your reward.
